description: A Taoist architectural complex from the Ming Dynasty, dedicated to Lü Dongbin, featuring exquisite architecture and profound cultural heritage.
China's first museum specializing in bronze, showcasing a collection of exquisite bronze culture artifacts.
A hub for traditional Taiyuan cuisine, gathering Shanxi specialty snacks and local flavors.
The riverside landscape belt of Taiyuan's mother river, featuring waterfront green spaces, ideal for leisurely walks and experiencing the modern charm of Taiyuan.
An ancient Buddhist temple with a serene environment, a great destination for hiking and worship.

attraction_name: Taiyuan Changfeng Cultural and Business District
description: Taiyuan's modern business core area, offering beautiful night views, perfect for leisurely strolls.
A Qing Dynasty bank building showcasing the development of traditional finance, contrasting with the Rishengchang Draft Bank.
A historical and cultural district in Taiyuan, combining traditional architecture with modern commerce to experience Jin merchants' culture.
A museum based on the former Shanxi Military Governor's Office, dedicated to showcasing the history of Jin merchants' culture.
A Ming Dynasty ancient temple with well-preserved Buddhist architecture, renowned for its exquisite painted sculptures and murals.
